claims.claim: The Pentagon added Unitree to its Section 1260H list of Chinese military companies on 2026-06-08, alongside Alibaba, Baidu and BYD; the list names 188 entities.
The Unitree G1 humanoid's official US list price is $13,500, down from a $16,000 launch price in May 2024.
Unitree launched the R1 humanoid in July 2025 at $5,900 (39,999 yuan), the cheapest mass-produced humanoid then available.
Unitree revenue rose from 159M yuan (2023) to 392M yuan (2024) to 1.70B yuan (2025), a ~335% YoY jump in 2025.
Unitree's humanoid gross margin fell from 87.67% (2023) to 68.44% (2024) to 62.91% (Jan-Sep 2025) as average selling prices collapsed ~72%.
Of Unitree's 2025 humanoid shipments, ~74% went to universities/research and only ~3-4% to genuinely productive enterprise use (inspection, reception).
Independent tracker Omdia ranked AgiBot #1 in 2025 humanoid shipments (5,168 units) ahead of Unitree (4,200) and UBTech (~1,000), out of 13,318 global units.
Unitree filed for a STAR Market IPO around 2026-03-20 seeking ~4.2B yuan (~$610M), with CITIC Securities as sponsor and a reported ~$7B valuation target.

Quasi-direct-drive actuators use a low-ratio gearbox (under ~10:1) and originated in the MIT Cheetah lineage; the MIT paper notes that for slow load-carrying a higher gear ratio is energetically superior.
Tesla Optimus and Figure use harmonic-drive (rotary) and planetary roller-screw (linear) actuators, not quasi-direct-drive, choosing the expensive high-torque-density path.
The Unitree G1 has a ~2 kg arm payload, far below the 15-25 kg of typical warehouse totes.
Tesla has shipped zero Optimus units to external customers; Musk conceded in the Q4 2025 call that Optimus is 'not in usage in our factories in a material way.'
Figure AI raised over $1B in Series C at a $39B post-money valuation (Sep 2025); its BMW Spartanburg deployment was a two-robot pilot, retired after ~1,250 runtime hours.
China controls roughly 69% of rare-earth mining, ~91% of refining, ~94% of NdFeB magnet production, and ~99% of heavy-rare-earth (Dy/Tb) separation.
China imposed export licensing on seven medium/heavy rare earths and their magnets on 2025-04-04, the dopants (Dy/Tb) that humanoid actuators need for heat resistance.
DJI was effectively locked out of the US market: CBP detains its imports under UFLPA (since Oct 2024) and the FCC added foreign-made drones to its Covered List on 2025-12-22.
A wormable Bluetooth vulnerability (UniPwn) disclosed September 2025 affects Unitree Go2/G1/H1/B2 robots via a single fleet-wide hardcoded AES key, enabling self-spreading root remote-code-execution.
Morgan Stanley projects ~1 billion humanoids and a $5 trillion market by 2050; Goldman Sachs projects a $38B market and 1.4M units by 2035 — forecasts that disagree by orders of magnitude.
Roboticist Rodney Brooks argues profitable humanoid deployment with even minimal dexterity is more than ten years away because dexterity cannot be learned from vision alone.

Unitree earmarked roughly $300M of IPO proceeds for in-house embodied foundation models and has open-sourced two (UnifoLM-WMA-0, UnifoLM-VLA-0), while also serving as an NVIDIA Isaac GR00T reference platform.
UBTech (HK:9880) began Walker S2 mass production in Nov 2025 with 2025 order book exceeding 800M yuan across automakers including BYD, Geely and Foxconn.
TrendForce projects Unitree and AgiBot together will capture nearly 80% of China humanoid shipments in 2026, indicating a duopoly rather than a single dominant firm.
Agility Robotics' Digit, working under the first commercial humanoid RaaS contract (GXO, June 2024), surpassed 100,000 totes moved in commercial operation; Digit is a ~$150K-class machine.

Harmonic Drive Systems (6324.T) stock more than doubled over the 12 months to June 2026 (from ~2,906 to ~6,240 yen, peaking ~7,800), as the market bid up the strain-wave incumbent on humanoid actuator demand.
BYD's NEV deliveries jumped from ~179K (2020) to ~604K (2021) after the Blade Battery, but BYD is shut out of the US by 100%+ tariffs with ~77% of 2025 sales still in China.
